title: elymus gmelinii var. macrantherus
intro: Elymus gmelinii var. macrantherus, also known as the Mongolian wildrye, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cough and cold symptoms, and promoting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Elymus gmelinii var. macrantherus is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
